rulings.Case Title: GLASS MOUNTAIN CAPITAL INC VS. MICHAEL CARSWELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 3, 2011, Line 1, Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Plaintiff Glass Mountain Capital¿s objection to claim of exemption. The exemption is granted if Judgment Debtor is able to present evidence at the hearing that the money held by the Sheriff is earnings paid within 30 days before the writ of execution was levied on 6/9/11 and that the Judgment Debtor is subject to an earnings withholding order for child support. Additionally, Judgment Debtor is ordered to pay $100 per month until the judgment is satisfied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA VINCENT VS. FIRST REPUBLIC BANK, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Deft Bank Of America , N.A., Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set Two, Request For Production Of Docs Set Three, And Form Interrogatories-General ,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 3, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF MELISSA VINCENT'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Bank Of America , N.A.'s Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set Two; Request For Production Of Documents Set Three, And Form Interrogatories-General. Plaintiff Melissa Vincent¿s motion to compel further discovery responses is denied. The quantity of discovery propounded by plaintiff is unreasonable given the nature of the case, unduly burdensome and contrary to the fundamental principles of an orderly system of adjudicating disputes, which require that all parties strive to resolve their disputes in as cost-effective manner as possible. All sanctions requests are denied. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA VINCENT VS. FIRST REPUBLIC BANK, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities ; Declarations Of Courtney Mardula, Brooke A. Brown, Matthew A. Goodin, Vols 1,2,3; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 3, 2011, Line 2. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO FIRST REPUBLIC BANKI, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant First Republic Bank¿s motion for protective order is granted. The quantity of discovery propounded by plaintiff is unreasonable given the nature of the case, unduly burdensome and contrary to the fundamental principles of an orderly system of adjudicating disputes, which require that all parties strive to resolve their disputes in as cost-effective manner as possible. Defendant is awarded sanctions in the amount of $2000 (an amount sufficient to bring this motion) against plaintiff¿s counsel. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: SAL ROSSELLI et al VS. SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, October 3, 2011, line 7, DEFENDANTS SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, ANDY STERN, ANNA BURGER, MARY HENRY, DAVID REGAN, ELISEO MEDINA, UHW-W MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is granted in part. Defendants are awarded all of the expenses they seek, but only 35% of the attorneys¿ fees they seek. Fees and costs are recoverable under the anti-SLAPP statute even if the dismissed claims were brought under the Ralph and Bane Acts. Defendants are entitled to be made whole, which is what the fees award does. The dollar amount of the fees sought for both the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion are not reasonable given the nature of the work involved. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. "Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests." =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DIONISIO VS. FOCIL-MB, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 3, 2011, line 13, DEFENDANT JOSEPH J. ALBANESE, INC. Motion To Strike. Defendant Joseph Albanese¿s motion to strike is granted as to the attorney¿s fees request and denied as to punitive damages and remedial conduct. The complaint adequately alleges that defendant acted in conscious disregard of the safety of bicyclists. Further information can be obtained through discovery. The remedial conduct allegation is potentially relevant for purposes other than to prove negligence. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: BRENDA J. COTTON VS. JOANN WARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 3, 2011, line 17, DEFENDANT JOANN WARE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Joann Ware¿s demurrer to first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to entire amended complaint because plaintiff fails to state a cause of action. The language of exhibit 3 indicates that plaintiff did not comply with the notice requirements of San Francisco Administrative Code 37.99(c). Exhibit 3 states that plaintiff only provided notice that advice is available regarding the amended complaint. Plaintiff has not shown that she can allege in good faith that the notice requirements of the ordinance were provided on or before the date of service of the notices. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
